candidates.title: Chase UK doubles cashback to 2% but makes it behaviour-conditional (15 transactions/month + £1,000 in savings)
candidates.why_product_decision: A major UK bank competitor repositions cashback as an engagement-conditioned reward (qualify via activity, not pay-in) — a live benchmark for how banks fund and gate rewards, and for engagement-mechanics design in offer products.
candidates.notes: New qualifying criteria start 1 June 2026; 2% rate (cap raised £15→£20/month) from 1 July. Covered by MSE in May and again in early June (https://www.moneysavingexpert.com/news/2026/6/chase-cashback-criteria-change/). Date used is the criteria-start date inside the window.

candidates.title: Visa Flexible Credential goes live in the UK on Zilch cards (with Thredd)
candidates.why_product_decision: One credential carrying multiple funding/payment options that can be 'financed, optimised and rewarded in real time' changes where rewards logic attaches to a UK card product — issuer-side flexibility the reader's offers layer may need to follow.
candidates.notes: Announced 12 May 2026 — one day before the window opens; first UK deployment of VFC, flagged as near-window.

candidates.title: Cardlytics hit with Nasdaq minimum-bid deficiency, executes 1-for-10 reverse stock split
candidates.why_product_decision: The largest US bank-channel CLO platform is under visible structural pressure — relevant to competitive positioning and to bank conversations about platform/partner durability, though the story itself is market-cap news.
candidates.notes: Likely rejects up (valuation/listing story) under the double-reject gate; at most an Also-moving line on competitor health. Deficiency notice 3 June; split effective 5 June, split-adjusted trading from 8 June.

candidates.title: Klarna Q1 2026: GMV up 33% to $33.7bn, revenue $1bn, as it scales its everyday-banking push
candidates.why_product_decision: An adjacent commerce-surface player (with UK P2P, Google Pay distribution and merchant offers) keeps compounding — context for who else owns the redemption moment the reader's bank clients compete for.
candidates.notes: Earnings story — likely rejects up unless used as one line evidencing competitor scaling; the product moves (P2P launch 14 Jan, Google Pay UK 16 Feb) are out of window.

candidates.title: Nationwide confirms 2026 member reward payout (£100 per eligible member) at 21 May annual results
candidates.why_product_decision: A top UK current-account brand keeps choosing direct cash distribution over merchant-funded offers — a benchmark for how UK banks express 'loyalty value' and what offer products are implicitly compared against.
candidates.notes: Confirm exact figures against Nationwide's results statement before use; secondary source is a consumer-finance roundup.

candidates.title: Asda confirms Ocado Smart Platform partnership for its online grocery operation from 2027
candidates.why_product_decision: Re-platforms one of the UK's largest grocery digital estates (700k+ orders/week) — shifts where grocery offers, loyalty mechanics and retail media will surface for a major merchant ecosystem the reader's clients fund offers into.
candidates.notes: Deal announced late May 2026; date given is the in-window roundup publication. See also IGD analysis: https://www.igd.com/articles/asda-and-ocado-what-the-deal-means-for-uk-online-grocery/73302

candidates.title: Tesco lets Clubcard vouchers be exchanged 1:1 to pay for travel insurance
candidates.why_product_decision: Clubcard widening redemption into financial-services products shows UK loyalty leaders competing on redemption breadth — a benchmark for the redemption catalogues bank rewards programmes are measured against.
candidates.notes: DATE APPROXIMATE — article appeared around late May 2026 but exact publication date not confirmed in research; verify before citing.

candidates.title: Co-op launches Media Network Out of Home: 1,000+ front-of-store digital screens with Smart Outdoor
candidates.why_product_decision: UK retail media keeps annexing new inventory (now neighbourhood DOOH, making Co-op the UK's fourth-largest digital outdoor operator) — the merchant ad budgets bank media channels and CLO programmes pitch for are being competed for at street level.
candidates.notes: Announced 14 May 2026, in-window. Confirmed via fetch: 1,000+ screens, 23 previously underserved territories, Smart Outdoor handles planning/buying.

candidates.title: Cardlytics Q1 call: three publishers now live on Cardlytics Rewards Platform as CLO supply moves beyond banks
candidates.why_product_decision: The bank-channel CLO incumbent is actively diversifying its publisher base beyond financial institutions — challenges the assumption that bank channels are the privileged surface for card-linked offers, and reframes exclusivity conversations with bank clients.
candidates.notes: Six days before window opens — flag if used. CRP itself launched May 2025; the in-window-adjacent delta is three live non-bank publishers with more in discussion.

candidates.title: PayPal Curated Ads: closed-loop purchase attribution extended to connected TV and open web
candidates.why_product_decision: A payments company monetising its transaction graph off its own surfaces shows where bank/fintech media economics are heading — transaction-verified attribution is the differentiator the reader's commerce-media proposition trades on.
candidates.notes: PRE-WINDOW (22 April) and US-focused; include only if framed as background to a bank-as-media trend with in-window UK anchors, otherwise drop.

candidates.title: Commerce media set to surpass TV ad spend as US upfronts begin
candidates.why_product_decision: The macro budget shift (WPP Media: commerce media $178bn vs TV $171bn, 15.6% of global ad spend) underwrites the entire bank-as-media-channel pitch the reader's company makes to banks and advertisers.
candidates.notes: Published 6 May, one week pre-window, and built on WPP Media's December 2025 end-of-year forecast — usable only as context, not as in-window movement. US upfronts framing; flag accordingly.

candidates.title: UKPI commercial VRP scheme goes live — the UK's first new payment scheme since Faster Payments (2008)
candidates.why_product_decision: Recurring account-to-account payments now have a commercial scheme with a single rulebook across all major UK banks — more UK spend will settle outside card rails, where card-linked offers cannot read the transaction. The A2A coverage gap becomes operational, not theoretical.
candidates.notes: Live 2 June 2026 (Wave 1: regulated/trusted sectors; Wave 2 e-commerce H2 2026). 31 firms in UKPI incl. all major UK retail banks. Also covered: https://www.openbankingexpo.com/news/uk-payments-initiative-launches-new-a2a-payments-scheme-in-defining-moment/

candidates.title: GoCardless launches Recurring Pay by Bank on the new UKPI scheme, targeting the card 'duopoly'
candidates.why_product_decision: Subscription and repeat spend — prime territory for retention-oriented offers — starts shifting to A2A rails that are invisible to card-linked measurement; GoCardless explicitly frames it against Visa/Mastercard's £1.5bn UK fee take.
candidates.notes: In-window, 2 June 2026, tied to UKPI launch.

candidates.title: TrueLayer launches Bank on File; IG Group, Trading 212, InvestEngine and East Lothian Housing Association live
candidates.why_product_decision: A card-on-file equivalent for bank payments rebuilds the stored-credential relationship — the exact place where card-linked loyalty attaches today — without a card in the loop.
candidates.notes: Date approximate (announced alongside/after UKPI go-live on 2 June); verify exact day. Named live merchants make this concrete rather than a roadmap claim.

candidates.title: Visa Payments Forum: Agent Score, Agentic Directory and Large Transaction Model announced for agentic commerce
candidates.why_product_decision: Visa is building the verification and readiness layer for agent-led checkout (merchant agent-readiness scoring, a directory of verified agents/merchants) — offers and rewards will need to be discoverable and verifiable in that layer to exist in the agentic surface.
candidates.notes: 10 June 2026, in-window. Global network announcement, though launched at a US forum. Also: stablecoin settlement at ~$7bn annualised run rate.

candidates.title: Visa and OpenAI partner to embed Visa payments across OpenAI's agentic commerce experiences
candidates.why_product_decision: The biggest card network plumbing credentials into ChatGPT-scale commerce fixes where the agentic checkout will sit — loyalty value must surface to the agent (not the human) at that moment, and nothing in the announcement covers offers yet.
candidates.notes: 10 June 2026, in-window. Pairs with Mastercard AP4M same day — the month's clearest agentic-commerce delta.

candidates.title: Mastercard launches Agent Pay for Machines (AP4M) with 30+ partners including Stripe, Adyen and Checkout.com
candidates.why_product_decision: Credentialed, permissioned machine-to-machine payments at high frequency and micro-value break the assumptions loyalty economics are built on (human-paced, human-chosen card transactions) — accrual and offer-targeting models don't yet have an answer for machine-velocity spend.
candidates.notes: Announced 10 June 2026 (confirmed via The Paypers same-day coverage). Press release does not mention rewards/offers — the silence is itself the signal. Multi-rail settlement: cards, accounts, stablecoins.

candidates.title: Apple WWDC: iOS 27 Wallet scans any physical loyalty card into Apple Wallet; Tap to Share passes loyalty data to merchants
candidates.why_product_decision: The OS-level wallet is becoming the default container for loyalty (camera-scan ingestion of any card, tap-to-share loyalty identity in-store) — it competes directly with the banking app as the surface where offers live and get redeemed.
candidates.notes: WWDC ran 8–12 June 2026; features ship with iOS 27 in September. Global platform change, not US-only. Apple newsroom services post: https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/06/apple-unveils-innovative-features-and-intelligence-experiences-across-services/

candidates.title: Financial Services and Markets Bill 2026-27 introduced: PSR functions to be folded into the FCA
candidates.why_product_decision: The regulator running both card-fee market reviews (including the cross-border interchange cap that shapes UK CLO economics) is changing hands — fee-remedy timing, engagement strategy and who the reader's compliance conversations happen with all shift.
candidates.notes: Bill introduced to Parliament / first reading in the Lords 19 May 2026; second reading 8 June 2026 — both in-window. Price-control powers carry over to the FCA. UK-first frame: this is the live UK interchange thread, not a US 'rewards funding squeeze'.

candidates.title: PSR annual plan 2026/27 makes implementing card fee remedies a top-three priority
candidates.why_product_decision: Confirms the cross-border interchange cap and scheme-fee remedies stay on track through the FCA transition — the cost base under merchant-funded and interchange-adjacent reward models gets its trajectory set here.
candidates.notes: PRE-WINDOW (published 29 April 2026); covered in the May 2026 payments newsletter. Use only as supporting context to the FSM Bill item, or drop. High Court upheld PSR cap powers 15 Jan 2026 (background).

candidates.title: FCA reopens AI Input Zone seeking evidence of good and poor AI practice; window closes 19 June
candidates.why_product_decision: The FCA is collecting the evidence base for its 2026 good/poor practice report on AI in financial services — AI-driven personalisation of offers and rewards in bank channels will be judged against it, and the contribution window is open now.
candidates.notes: FCA blog: https://www.fca.org.uk/news/blogs/ai-financial-services-approach. Submission deadline 19 June 2026 is inside the window — time-sensitive.

candidates.title: FCA AI Live Testing second cohort underway — Barclays, Lloyds (Scottish Widows), Experian among eight firms; use cases include agentic payments
candidates.why_product_decision: UK banks are testing agentic payments and customer-facing AI inside an FCA-supervised sandbox — the regulatory pathway for agent-mediated bank experiences (where offers would surface) is being defined with named UK banks in it.
candidates.notes: PRE-WINDOW (cohort announced 22 April; testing began April, runs to year-end). Include only as context to the FCA AI Input Zone item or to an agentic-commerce trend's UK leg.

candidates.title: FCA open finance push: mortgages and open finance policy sprint scheduled for June 2026 under the Smart Data Accelerator
candidates.why_product_decision: Open finance is moving from roadmap to use-case workshops — the data-sharing framework that would let offer personalisation draw on wider financial data (and let competitors do the same) is being shaped in-window.
candidates.notes: Roadmap itself published pre-window; the in-window element is the June 2026 policy sprint. Date is approximate (sprint timed 'June 2026') — verify before citing a specific day. FCA roadmap: https://www.fca.org.uk/publications/corporate-documents/open-finance-roadmap
